mnemonics: Naive BAYES: Because Assumptions Yield Effective Simplicity
keyPoints: The model is based on applying Bayes' Theorem with a strong (naive) assumption of independence between every pair of features given the class label.
It calculates the posterior probability for each class and then classifies the instance to the class with the highest posterior probability.
Despite its simplistic assumptions, it often performs well, especially in text classification and spam filtering.
commonMistakes: Confusing the prior probability P(Class) with the posterior probability P(Class | Features).
Forgetting the 'naive' assumption of feature independence, which is rarely true in real-world data but is key to the model's simplicity and efficiency.
